summary: Implemented a lightweight M7 player guidance panel in the player shell with localized six-step guidance for first objective, district selection, inspection, governance preview, result observation, and next-step feedback. The panel is collapsible, dismissible/restorable, consumes existing read-model state and M7 guidance evidence, and does not submit commands or mutate simulation state.
head_commit_note: This handoff is committed with the implementation; the exact final branch head is reported after commit by git rev-parse HEAD.

tests_run.result: Showed only this task's modified UI, Storybook, test, and handoff files before commit.
Listed tasks; M7-PLAYER-GUIDANCE-LITE-001 remained IN_PROGRESS at validation time.
Reported M7-SAVE-MIGRATION-CLOUD-BOUNDARIES-001 as next ready task.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M7-PLAYER-GUIDANCE-LITE-001
tests_run.result: task file shape ok M7-PLAYER-GUIDANCE-LITE-001 IN_PROGRESS client_engineer qa_reviewer 6 14
All M1/M2/M3/M6/M7 content fixtures validated; M7 beta scenario-person-event manifest hash 92aed91e.
Vite web build passed; existing chunk-size warning remained.
Storybook build passed; existing Vite chunk-size and plugin timing warnings remained.
Playwright passed: 21/21 tests, including English, zh-CN, system fallback, collapse, dismiss, and appointment-path guidance checks.
format:check, lint, typecheck, and architecture boundary checks passed.

decisions: Kept guidance as React local UI state only; dismiss/collapse does not write simulation state or client read models.
Consumed existing read-model presence, appointment flow state, command status, and M7 tutorial/hint/encyclopedia evidence instead of creating a new tutorial system.
Kept command submission in existing preview/confirm controls; guidance never auto-submits commands and explicitly reminds that command costs and rejection reasons stay visible in action panels.

Kept raw M7 tutorial/hints/encyclopedia workspace behind existing debug mode; player guidance uses localized generic player text and does not expose raw ids, raw reason codes, state hash, revision, milestone labels, or prototype labels.
Default guidance state remains review-aware because the current M7 content pack still carries review blockers; guidance stays usable while warning against irreversible choices.
scope_notes: Modified only allowed paths under apps/web, packages/ui, tests, .storybook, and project/messages.
No packages/sim-core, protocol, save-format, map-renderer, platform, Electron, docs, status, or CI files were modified.
No new dependency, server, telemetry, cloud-save, account, release, branding, or manual node battle scope was introduced.
Historical and cultural wording is generic and review-aware; unresolved content risk remains routed by existing M7 review blockers rather than invented in UI text.
risks: M7 guidance defaults to review-aware warning state because formal content lock acceptance is still outside this task.
Vite and Storybook continue to emit pre-existing chunk-size warnings; build commands exit successfully.
